summary: Sislib decompilation cleanup and matching-progress PR touching src/sysdolphin/baselib/sislib.c and sislib.h. The diff replaces generated-looking dead locals, gotos, disabled #if 0 blocks, and unclear pointer/byte handling with more structured loops, explicit buffers, cursor pointers, typed casts, recovered helper bodies, and an updated HSD_SisLib_803A7684 prototype. Bot report records score improvements for seven unmatched sislib items, but the slice has no human review comments and no PR body details.

key_files.role: Primary sislib implementation changes: rewrites HSD_SisLib_803A67EC and HSD_SisLib_803A6B98, adds/reworks HSD_SisLib_803A70A0, HSD_SisLib_803A7684, HSD_SisLib_803A7F0C, and adjusts HSD_SisLib_803A8134/HSD_SisLib_803A84BC call and cursor handling.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: sislib.c +716/-410, 16 hunks
raw_diff: HSD_SisLib_803A67EC replaces loop_42/loop_37 gotos and many dead_r* locals with for/do-while loops, u8* LUT pointers, and out_ptr
raw_diff: HSD_SisLib_803A6B98 replaces dead_sp/dead_r locals with buffer[256], encoded[128], old_buf, cur, bulk_src/tail_src, and direct x/y coordinate handling
raw_diff: HSD_SisLib_803A70A0 added after fn_803A6FEC with formatted text encoding, grow/shrink buffer handling, and bulk/tail copies
raw_diff: HSD_SisLib_803A8134 PAD_STACK changed from 16 to 0x8 and cursor/typed u16/s16 reads adjusted
raw_diff: HSD_SisLib_803A84BC now calls HSD_SisLib_803A8134 for opcode 7 and passes sis_cursor pointers to HSD_SisLib_803A7684

key_files.role: Header prototype update for recovered HSD_SisLib_803A7684 signature.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: sislib.h +1/-1, 1 hunk
raw_diff: HSD_SisLib_803A7684 prototype changed from void HSD_SisLib_803A7684(UNK_T, u8, u8) to void HSD_SisLib_803A7684(HSD_Text*, u8*, u8)

what_changed: Updated match-status comments for HSD_SisLib_803A5798 and HSD_SisLib_803A594C, documenting remaining register allocation/scheduling and literal pooling issues rather than only generic register allocation notes.

Rewrote HSD_SisLib_803A67EC from a goto-heavy, partially disabled/decompiler-shaped form into structured input scanning and LUT lookup loops, with dead_r* temporaries removed and byte-pointer LUT/output handling made explicit.

Reworked HSD_SisLib_803A6B98 to use explicit local formatting and encoded buffers, va_list handling, old/new SIS buffer growth, byte-copy loops, cursor-style output writes through alloc->data_0, and normal x/y coordinate casts.

Added HSD_SisLib_803A70A0, which locates an existing SIS entry, encodes formatted replacement text, grows or shrinks the backing buffer, moves tail data with bulk/tail loops, and writes the new encoded payload.

Changed HSD_SisLib_803A8134 control/cursor handling, including typed u16/s16 reads, corrected HSD_SisLib_803A7684 pointer arguments, a smaller PAD_STACK value, and a while loop for clearing string_buffer tail bytes.
Replaced an inline helper shape around HSD_SisLib_803A7F0C with concrete HSD_SisLib_803A7684 and HSD_SisLib_803A7F0C bodies that manage style-state stack entries in text->string_buffer.
Updated HSD_SisLib_803A84BC command parsing to use typed multi-byte reads, call HSD_SisLib_803A8134 for line measurement, and pass cursor pointers into HSD_SisLib_803A7684.
Updated sislib.h so HSD_SisLib_803A7684 has the recovered signature HSD_Text*, u8*, u8 instead of UNK_T, u8, u8.

smart_moves.text: Kept unresolved matching facts in @todo comments for HSD_SisLib_803A5798 and HSD_SisLib_803A594C, naming the specific remaining regalloc/scheduling and literal-pooling issues instead of implying full closure.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: updated @todo comments at HSD_SisLib_803A5798 and HSD_SisLib_803A594C

smart_moves.text: Removed numerous dead_r*/dead_sp* temporaries and disabled generated blocks in favor of role-named locals such as encoded, old_buf, cur, bulk_src, tail_src, lut_ptr, out_ptr, and copy_idx.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: HSD_SisLib_803A67EC and HSD_SisLib_803A6B98 local variable rewrites

smart_moves.text: Updated the owning header when HSD_SisLib_803A7684's argument types became clearer.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: sislib.h prototype hunk

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a decompiler-shaped loop uses labels/gotos and dead temporaries but the operation is an index-bounded scan or LUT walk, test natural for/do-while forms before preserving generated control flow.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: HSD_SisLib_803A67EC loop_42 replaced with for (in_idx = 0; in_idx < 0x80; in_idx++, str_cursor++)
raw_diff: HSD_SisLib_803A67EC loop_37 replaced with do/while LUT scan over lbl_8040C8C0

decomp_lessons.lesson: For byte-oriented SIS buffers, recovered cursor pointers and explicit u8 buffers can make the source clearer than single-byte pseudo-locals and stack-dead temporaries, while still preserving byte-copy loop structure where needed.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: HSD_SisLib_803A6B98 introduces buffer[256], encoded[128], u8** cur, and bulk/tail copy pointers
raw_diff: HSD_SisLib_803A70A0 uses encoded[128], playhead, src/dst, tail_src, bulk_count, and remainder

decomp_lessons.lesson: When function-body work proves an address-style helper signature, update the exported prototype rather than leaving UNK_T or stale argument types.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: HSD_SisLib_803A7684 definition takes HSD_Text* text, u8* cursor, u8 flags
raw_diff: sislib.h prototype updated to void HSD_SisLib_803A7684(HSD_Text*, u8*, u8)

naming_conventions.name: Prefer role-based locals over dead register/stack names once the role is clear.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: dead_r*/dead_sp* locals removed in HSD_SisLib_803A67EC and HSD_SisLib_803A6B98

naming_conventions.name: Keep unresolved address-style function names when semantic names are not proven, while improving parameter and local names.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: address-style HSD_SisLib_* function names retained while locals and prototypes are improved

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use structured byte-copy loops with bulk groups of 8 plus tail loops when source needs to preserve observed copy shape.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: HSD_SisLib_803A6B98 copies encoded bytes with an 8-byte loop then tail loop
raw_diff: HSD_SisLib_803A70A0 grow/shrink paths and payload write use bulk_count/remainder loops
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The slice shows the tactic in code, but does not include objdiff output proving each loop form's necessity.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use pointer-to-cursor writes to model advancing SIS output streams.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: HSD_SisLib_803A6B98 sets cur = (u8**) &alloc->data_0 and writes *(*cur)++ values
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: This is PR evidence of the chosen shape, not a global endorsement for all cursor-writing code.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use PAD_STACK only narrowly and adjust its size when local matching requires it.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: HSD_SisLib_803A8134 changes PAD_STACK(16) to PAD_STACK(0x8)
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The diff contains the PAD_STACK change, but the loaded slice does not include narrow build/objdiff evidence for the stack padding.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Document known residual non-matching causes in comments when landing partial-score progress.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: HSD_SisLib_803A5798 comment lists HSD_SisLib_804D7970 pointer register and empty-string literal pooling
raw_diff: HSD_SisLib_803A594C comment lists coalesce-block register allocation/scheduling differences
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: These comments are author-provided evidence in the diff; the slice does not independently validate them.

notes: Strong evidence for changed files, concrete code-shape changes, header prototype update, and bot-reported score improvements.
Weak evidence for reviewer intent because there are no human review comments or review records in the loaded slice.
The PR body is empty, so intent and validation details mainly come from the diff and bot report.
The loaded slice includes score improvements but not local build logs, objdiff details, or regression output; do not treat any function as fully matched unless explicitly supported elsewhere.
Some tactics, especially PAD_STACK and specific regalloc/literal-pooling explanations, are visible in the diff but lack independent verification in the slice.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Replace generated goto/dead-temp loops with natural loops when the byte-scan or LUT pattern is clear
accepted_candidate_records.text: In sislib, HSD_SisLib_803A67EC moved from label/goto and dead_r* temporaries to a bounded for loop over the input string and a do/while loop over a two-byte LUT. This supports the reusable lesson that clear byte scans and LUT walks should be tested as natural loops before preserving generated control flow.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: HSD_SisLib_803A67EC loop_42 and loop_37 rewrites

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recover prototypes in headers when helper bodies prove argument types
accepted_candidate_records.text: The PR changes HSD_SisLib_803A7684 from UNK_T/u8/u8 to HSD_Text*/u8*/u8 in sislib.h after the implementation and call sites use an HSD_Text pointer and cursor pointer. This is a concrete sislib example of keeping exported prototypes truthful.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: HSD_SisLib_803A7684 definition and sislib.h prototype update

accepted_candidate_records.title: Bulk-plus-tail byte-copy loops appeared repeatedly in sislib buffer movement
accepted_candidate_records.text: HSD_SisLib_803A6B98 and HSD_SisLib_803A70A0 use groups-of-8 copy loops followed by tail loops for encoded bytes and buffer tail movement. This is useful search context for sislib matching, but should still require local objdiff evidence before applying broadly.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: HSD_SisLib_803A6B98 bulk/tail encoded copy
raw_diff: HSD_SisLib_803A70A0 grow/shrink and payload copy loops

rejection_notes: Do not promote the bot score improvements as full matches; the report only says percentage improvements for unmatched items.
Do not infer human reviewer approval or requested changes; no human review comments are present.
Do not make PAD_STACK(0x8) a general sislib rule from this slice alone; the diff shows the tactic but not verifier evidence.
Do not promote the author comments about regalloc/literal pooling as independently validated global facts without build/objdiff corroboration.
